description: Integration tests in note_body_editor_handlers.rs bypass the actual editor_update_note_body Tauri command and manipulate NoteBodyStore directly via store.0.lock().unwrap(). PROP-006 (lines 38-86), PROP-007 (lines 93-132, 135-173), and PROP-010 (lines 180-210, 213-303, 259-303) all insert InMemoryNoteBody entries directly into the HashMap instead of calling the Tauri command through the IPC layer. This means the tests do not verify: (a) the actual IPC wire contract (argument names, serialization), (b) that validate_no_control_chars is invoked through the command path, (c) that the Tauri State injection works correctly, (d) that event emission (editing_session_state_changed) works when hooked up. These are not integration tests — they are unit tests that happen to live in a tests/ directory.
expected: Integration tests should invoke the actual Tauri command through the IPC layer (using tauri::test::assert_ipc_response or a Tauri test harness that exercises #[tauri::command] functions). At minimum, tests should call editor_update_note_body() directly as a Rust function (since it's pub) rather than bypassing it to manipulate the store.
